name: Prose Section
description: Long-form editorial or company-story section with comfortable reading width, optional supporting media, and a stable narrative rhythm for marketing and about pages.

standard.description: Single-column editorial section with overline, heading, body copy, and optional supporting media or quote.

slots.heading: Section heading with heading2 or heading3 scale
slots.body: Long-form prose blocks with comfortable line length and muted supporting text
slots.supporting-media: Optional image, pull quote, or stat block below the prose

story.description: Narrative company-story section with wider body copy, optional image pairing, and a sequential scroll rhythm that feels deliberate instead of dense.

slots.heading-column: Eyebrow, heading, and lead paragraph anchoring the story
slots.body-column: Narrative prose blocks, milestone list, or supporting quote stack
slots.media-column: Optional founder portrait, product still, or ambient visual proof aligned to the story

visual_brief: Editorial prose block that feels authored and breathable rather than like a utility text dump. Heading and lead copy establish narrative intent first, followed by long-form paragraphs with generous line-height and restrained width. The story preset can pair the prose with a portrait, ambient image, or milestone rail, but the text remains the primary visual element.

layout_hints.measure: Keep prose at a comfortable reading measure: roughly 60-75 characters per line for body copy. Do not let long-form copy run edge-to-edge across the viewport.
layout_hints.surface_usage: This pattern should not automatically wrap itself in a d-surface card. Treat it as editorial content living within the shell rhythm unless a route explicitly needs contained prose.
layout_hints.vertical_rhythm: Use a calm vertical rhythm between heading, lead, and body blocks. Paragraph groups should feel authored, not densely stacked utility text.
layout_hints.supporting_media: If supporting media is present, it should reinforce the story without overpowering the text column. Favor one strong visual rather than a collage.

responsive.mobile: Collapse to a single editorial column with tighter section spacing. Supporting media moves above or below the text rather than beside it. Preserve reading measure with comfortable horizontal padding.
responsive.tablet: Single-column by default with optional media below the prose. Story preset may begin introducing a two-region composition when space allows, but text should remain the focus.
responsive.desktop: Standard preset stays single-column with a max-width reading measure. Story preset can split into text and supporting-media columns with generous gap spacing.

motion.micro: Minimal reveal motion only: supporting media and quote accents may fade in softly, but prose should remain calm and stable.
motion.transitions: Section-level entrance may use a subtle fade-up or staggered reveal of prose blocks over 200-300ms, never a dramatic slide or parallax.
motion.ambient: Avoid ornamental ambient motion inside the prose section itself; let surrounding page treatments carry atmosphere.

keyboard: Tab moves between links, CTA buttons, and interactive supporting content in reading order.
announcements: Screen readers should encounter the heading before body copy and optional supporting media in source order.
accessibility.focus_management: Keep prose in semantic heading and paragraph structure. Interactive supporting elements should follow the narrative flow rather than interrupt it.

composition.ProseSection: Container(d-section, editorial rhythm) > [Eyebrow? + Heading + Lead? + Body + SupportingMedia?]
